notes: Monark is a meticulous Moog Model D emulation running inside Reaktor 6, built to capture the specific circuit behaviour and non-linearities of the original hardware. It features three oscillators with variable waveforms, a classic 24dB/octave transistor ladder filter with its distinctive resonant character, a single LFO, and an analog-style overdrive circuit. Monark excels at fat bass lines, lead tones, and evolving filter sweeps. The ladder filter's self-oscillation and non-linear saturation at high resonance are the instrument's most characteristic feature. CC 74 (Brightness) maps naturally to Filter Cutoff and is the primary expressive control alongside CC 11. CC 1 (Modulation) typically controls Filter Cutoff depth or LFO depth depending on the patch. Portamento/Glide (QC7) is essential for authentic lead playing — the original Model D's glide circuit is specifically emulated. Monark runs as a Reaktor ensemble, so Quick Control assignments are set within Reaktor's MIDI panel and may vary by preset.

Volume.description: Master output level of the Reaktor ensemble

Expression.description: Dynamic expression / output level — use for phrase shaping on lead or bass lines

Modulation.description: Filter Cutoff modulation depth or LFO depth depending on patch — mod wheel for filter sweeps

Sustain.description: Sustain / gate hold — holds notes in certain patch modes

Release Time.description: Envelope release time — controls how long the VCA/VCF envelope tails off

Attack Time.description: Envelope attack time — from instant percussive to slow pad-like attacks

Brightness.description: Filter Cutoff frequency — the primary tonal control; maps to the ladder filter cutoff

Reverb Send.description: Reverb wet level — if a reverb module is present in the ensemble

Quick Control 1.description: Filter Cutoff — main cutoff frequency of the 24dB ladder filter; most expressive real-time control

Quick Control 2.description: Filter Resonance / Emphasis — resonance amount; at maximum the filter self-oscillates

Quick Control 3.description: Oscillator Mix / Balance — relative level balance between the three oscillators

Quick Control 4.description: Filter Envelope Amount — how strongly the filter envelope modulates the cutoff frequency

Quick Control 5.description: LFO Rate — speed of the low-frequency oscillator modulation

Quick Control 6.description: LFO Depth — intensity of LFO modulation applied to the target (typically pitch or filter)

Quick Control 7.description: Portamento / Glide — glide time between notes; emulates the original Model D's glide circuit

Quick Control 8.description: Overdrive / Saturation — input saturation level for the characteristic Moog transistor clipping and warmth
